title: haplopappus scopulorum
intro: Haplopappus scopulorum, also known as the alpine sunflower or mountain sunflower, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is also valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. Haplopappus scopulorum can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
